clusters.title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison for Gilgo Beach serial killings
clusters.neutral_headline: Gilgo Beach killer sentenced to life without parole
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera, Guardian World

items.title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison for Gilgo Beach serial killings
items.standfirst: Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole for the series of murders known as the Gilgo Beach serial killings.
items.content: Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole for the series of murders known as the Gilgo Beach serial killings.

items.title: Families of victims of Gilgo Beach serial killer call him an evil monster in court
items.standfirst: In a Long Island courtroom Wednesday, Rex Heuermann received three life sentences for choking and dismembering eight women, most of them in his basement, over the course of more than 15 years. The 62-year-old addressed the court before he was sent away. Tom Hanson reports.
items.content: In a Long Island courtroom Wednesday, Rex Heuermann received three life sentences for choking and dismembering eight women, most of them in his basement, over the course of more than 15 years. The 62-year-old addressed the court before he was sent away. Tom Hanson reports.

items.title: Gilgo Beach killer sentenced to life without parole
items.standfirst: New York’s Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life without parole for the murders of eight women.
items.content: New York’s Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life without parole for the murders of eight women.

items.title: Families of eight women killed confront the Gilgo Beach serial killer: ‘Save a spot in hell’
items.standfirst: New York judge imposes life without parole after emotional statements at Rex Heuermann’s sentencingSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe families of eight women killed by Rex Heuermann spoke at the Gilgo Beach killer’s sentencing in Riverhead, New York, on Wednesday, more than three decades after the 62-year-old Manhattan architect began his killing spree.Heuermann pleaded guilty to murdering seven women and admitted to the killing of an eighth victim in April. Just before being sentenced to life in prison without parole on Wednesday for his admitted crimes, Heuermann offered a weak, generalized apology for his actions. Continue reading...
items.content: New York judge imposes life without parole after emotional statements at Rex Heuermann’s sentencingSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe families of eight women killed by Rex Heuermann spoke at the Gilgo Beach killer’s sentencing in Riverhead, New York, on Wednesday, more than three decades after the 62-year-old Manhattan architect began his killing spree.Heuermann pleaded guilty to murdering seven women and admitted to the killing of an eighth victim in April. Just before being sentenced to life in prison without parole on Wednesday for his admitted crimes, Heuermann offered a weak, generalized apology for his actions. Continue reading...

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, NPR News

items.title: Tropical Storm Arthur forms off Texas Gulf Coast
items.standfirst: Tropical Storm Arthur, the first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, formed Wednesday off the Gulf Coast of Texas.
items.content: Tropical Storm Arthur, the first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, formed Wednesday off the Gulf Coast of Texas.

items.title: Tropical Storm Arthur rolls through the Gulf
items.standfirst: Severe weather threatened more than 125 million Americans on Wednesday. Multiple states along the Gulf Coast issued flood alerts as Tropical Storm Arthur rolled through. Rob Marciano has the forecast.
items.content: Severe weather threatened more than 125 million Americans on Wednesday. Multiple states along the Gulf Coast issued flood alerts as Tropical Storm Arthur rolled through. Rob Marciano has the forecast.

items.title: Tropical Storm Arthur is the first named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season
items.standfirst: Forecasters say Arthur could generate life-threatening flash floods along the northern Gulf Coast. But it is not expected to strengthen further.
items.content: Forecasters say Arthur could generate life-threatening flash floods along the northern Gulf Coast. But it is not expected to strengthen further.

items.title: Fed keeps interest rates steady in first decision under Kevin Warsh

items.content: As was widely expected, the Federal Reserve unanimously voted to keep interest rates unchanged on Wednesday. CBS News coordinating producer Richard Escobedo has more details.

items.title: Federal Reserve holds rates steady but signals possible hike before year’s end
items.standfirst: Open markets committee says ‘economic activity is expanding at a solid pace’ in first meeting under new chair Kevin WarshUS stock markets dropped on Wednesday afternoon after the Federal Reserve left interest rates unchanged and signaled a possible rate hike before the end of the year.The Fed was widely expected to keep rates at a range of 3.5% to 3.75%, where they have remained since December. The decision was unanimously supported by the Fed’s voting committee. Continue reading...

The decision was unanimously supported by the Fed’s voting committee. Continue reading...

items.title: Kane double fires England past Croatia in World Cup thriller
items.standfirst: Harry Kane scores twice, while Jude Bellingham and Marcus Rashford also net, as England beat Croatia 4-2 in 2026 opener.
items.content: Harry Kane scores twice, while Jude Bellingham and Marcus Rashford also net, as England beat Croatia 4-2 in 2026 opener.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Kane, Bellingham and Rashford fire England past Croatia
items.standfirst: England claimed a convincing 4-2 victory over Croatia in an enthralling Group L encounter in Arlington, Texas, with Harry Kane, Jude Bellingham and Marcus Rashford combining to overcome a resilient Croatian comeback. The Three Lions survived sustained pressure from Zlatko Dalic's side in a match that lived up to its billing as a rematch of the 2018 World Cup semi-final.
items.content: England claimed a convincing 4-2 victory over Croatia in an enthralling Group L encounter in Arlington, Texas, with Harry Kane, Jude Bellingham and Marcus Rashford combining to overcome a resilient Croatian comeback. The Three Lions survived sustained pressure from Zlatko Dalic's side in a match that lived up to its billing as a rematch of the 2018 World Cup semi-final.

items.title: England - Croatia: Relive Three Lions' victory in their World Cup opener
items.standfirst: England began their World Cup campaign against Croatia with a 4-2 victory in a rematch of the 2018 semi-final. Thomas Tuchel's side put aside the pre-match selection controversy to claim three points in Group L. Follow the key moments from the match below.

clusters.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
clusters.neutral_headline: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
bullet_summary: Reported by Ars Technica News, Wired

items.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
items.standfirst: AI models with advanced hacking capabilities will soon be the norm.
items.content: Late last week, Anthropic took its new Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models offline following a United States government export-control directive barring “any foreign national” from using the services. The company has been in talks with the White House since Friday but has yet to secure an agreement that would allow it to reinstate the offerings. Since Mythos debuted in April, Anthropic has claimed—and warned—that the model has advanced capabilities for not only finding software vulnerabilities to help defenders patch them, but also figuring out ways to exploit them that could be used by bad actors. Anthropic itself noted this double-edged sword in its launch of Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5. “A great deal of advanced usage of AI models is dual use: the same queries that are beneficial in the hands of cybersecurity professionals and biology researchers could be dangerous if available to malicious actors,” the company wrote in a blog post last week. With this in mind, the company initially released a version called Mythos Preview to a select consortium as part of a working group known as Project Glasswing. Mythos 5 was also privately released to this group last week, while Claude Fable 5, which is a Mythos-grade model, was released to the general public with specific blocks on its ability to give responses to questions about biology and cybersecurity.Read full article Comments

items.title: ‘Dangerous’ AI Models Are Coming No Matter What
items.standfirst: The US government crackdown on Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 hides a glaring truth: AI models with advanced hacking capabilities will soon be the norm.
items.content: The US government crackdown on Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 hides a glaring truth: AI models with advanced hacking capabilities will soon be the norm.

clusters.title: Paramount rejected ad criticizing its owners and Warner Bros acquisition
clusters.neutral_headline: Paramount rejected ad criticizing its owners and Warner Bros acquisition
bullet_summary: The commercial was submitted by the Freedom of the Press Foundation to run during Donald Trump’s UFC event

items.title: Paramount rejected ad criticizing its owners and Warner Bros acquisition
items.standfirst: Rejected citing ‘conflict of interest’, the ad took aim at Trump allies David Ellison, Paramount Skydance chief, and his billionaire father, Larry EllisonParamount Skydance refused to air an ad submitted by a press freedom group that heavily criticized the network’s leadership and merger with Warner Bros Discovery, with an advertising associate deeming it a “conflict of interest”.The Freedom of the Press Foundation had hoped to air the 30-second ad during Sunday’s Ultimate Fighting Championship broadcast at the White House, which aired on the streaming service Paramount+ – though a client partner for Paramount told the organization’s ad-buyer that such placement was not guaranteed. Continue reading...
items.content: Rejected citing ‘conflict of interest’, the ad took aim at Trump allies David Ellison, Paramount Skydance chief, and his billionaire father, Larry EllisonParamount Skydance refused to air an ad submitted by a press freedom group that heavily criticized the network’s leadership and merger with Warner Bros Discovery, with an advertising associate deeming it a “conflict of interest”.The Freedom of the Press Foundation had hoped to air the 30-second ad during Sunday’s Ultimate Fighting Championship broadcast at the White House, which aired on the streaming service Paramount+ – though a client partner for Paramount told the organization’s ad-buyer that such placement was not guaranteed. Continue reading...

items.title: Paramount Refused to Air an Ad Criticizing Its Merger With Warner Bros.
items.standfirst: The commercial was submitted by the Freedom of the Press Foundation to run during Donald Trump’s UFC event. It criticized the $111 billion merger as a threat to the First Amendment.
items.content: The commercial was submitted by the Freedom of the Press Foundation to run during Donald Trump’s UFC event. It criticized the $111 billion merger as a threat to the First Amendment.
